    json { "CodeContent": "\"const puppeteer = require('/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/node_modules/puppeteer');\\n\\n(async () => {\\n const browser = await puppeteer.launch({\\n headless: true,\\n });\\n const page = await browser.newPage();\\n await page.goto('http://localhost:8000', { waitUntil: 'networkidle0' });\\n await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 3000));\\n\\n const buttons = await page.evaluate(() => {\\n return Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('button')).map(b => ({\\n id: b.id,\\n className: b.className,\\n type: b.getAttribute('type'),\\n html: b.outerHTML\\n }));\\n });\\n console.log(\\\"Found buttons:\\\", JSON.stringify(buttons, null, 2));\\n\\n await browser.close();\\n})();\\n\"", "Description": "\"Modify find_button.js to require puppeteer using an absolute path\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/.gemini/antigravity/brain/a7dd681c-7ee8-4f02-8fa7-224242ec388f/scratch/find_button.js\"", "toolAction": "\"Modify script\"", "toolSummary": "\"Modify scratch script\"" }

document.querySelector('textarea');\\n if (ta) {\\n ta.value = 'use arxiv and search for papers on H-LSTMs';\\n ta.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true }));\\n }\\n });\\n await page.screenshot({path: 'tests/manual/screenshots/chainlit2.png'});\\n \\n console.log(\\\"Submitting...\\\");\\n await page.evaluate(() => {\\n const btn = document.querySelector('#chat-submit');\\n     json { "CodeContent": "\"import os\\nimport requests\\nimport json\\n\\npk = os.getenv(\\\"LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY\\\")\\nsk = os.getenv(\\\"LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY\\\")\\nhost = os.getenv(\\\"LANGFUSE_HOST\\\", \\\"http://langfuse-server:3000\\\")\\n\\ntrace_id = \\\"305397354a286953f2f5568835da7219\\\"\\nr_detail = requests.get(f\\\"{host}/api/public/traces/{trace_id}\\\", auth=(pk, sk))\\ndetail = r_detail.json()\\nprint(\\\"Trace Level:\\\")\\nprint(f\\\" Name: {detail.get('name')}\\\")\\nprint(f\\\" Input: {detail.get('input')}\\\")\\nprint(f\\\" Output: {detail.get('output')}\\\")\\n\\nprint(\\\"\\\\nObservations:\\\")\\nfor obs in detail.get(\\\"observations\\\", []):\\n print(f\\\"- ID: {obs.get('id')}\\\")\\n print(f\\\" Name: {obs.get('name')}\\\")\\n print(f\\\" Type: {obs.get('type')}\\\")\\n print(f\\\" Input: {json.dumps(obs.get('input'))[:200]}\\\")\\n print(f\\\" Output: {json.dumps(obs.get('output'))[:200]}\\\")\\n\"", "Description": "\"Modify get_trace.py to inspect trace 305397354a286953f2f5568835da7219\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/.gemini/antigravity/brain/a7dd681c-7ee8-4f02-8fa7-224242ec388f/scratch/get_trace.py\"", "toolAction": "\"Modify script\"", "toolSummary": "\"Modify scratch script\"" }

    json { "CodeContent": "\"import os\\nimport requests\\nimport json\\n\\npk = os.getenv(\\\"LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY\\\")\\nsk = os.getenv(\\\"LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY\\\")\\nhost = os.getenv(\\\"LANGFUSE_HOST\\\", \\\"http://langfuse-server:3000\\\")\\n\\n# Fetch latest traces\\nr = requests.get(f\\\"{host}/api/public/traces\\\", auth=(pk, sk))\\ndata = r.json()\\nif \\\"data\\\" in data and len(data[\\\"data\\\"]) > 0:\\n for i, trace in enumerate(data[\\\"data\\\"][:5]):\\n print(f\\\"Index {i} - ID: {trace['id']} - Name: {trace.get('name')} - Input: {trace.get('input')}\\\")\\nelse:\\n print(\\\"No traces found!\\\")\\n\"", "Description": "\"Modify get_trace.py to print top 5 traces with name and input\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/.gemini/antigravity/brain/a7dd681c-7ee8-4f02-8fa7-224242ec388f/scratch/get_trace.py\"", "toolAction": "\"Modify script\"", "toolSummary": "\"Modify scratch script\"" }

    json { "CodeContent": "\"import os\\nimport requests\\nimport json\\n\\npk = os.getenv(\\\"LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY\\\")\\nsk = os.getenv(\\\"LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY\\\")\\nhost = os.getenv(\\\"LANGFUSE_HOST\\\", \\\"http://langfuse-server:3000\\\")\\n\\n# Fetch latest traces\\nr = requests.get(f\\\"{host}/api/public/traces\\\", auth=(pk, sk))\\ndata = r.json()\\nif \\\"data\\\" in data and len(data[\\\"data\\\"]) > 0:\\n for i, trace in enumerate(data[\\\"data\\\"][:10]):\\n print(f\\\"Index {i} - ID: {trace['id']} - Name: {trace.get('name')} - Timestamp: {trace.get('timestamp')} - Input: {trace.get('input')}\\\")\\nelse:\\n print(\\\"No traces found!\\\")\\n\"", "Description": "\"Modify get_trace.py to print top 10 traces with name, timestamp and input\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/.gemini/antigravity/brain/a7dd681c-7ee8-4f02-8fa7-224242ec388f/scratch/get_trace.py\"", "toolAction": "\"Modify script\"", "toolSummary": "\"Modify scratch script\"" }

    json { "AllowMultiple": "false", "Description": "\"Update debug_chainlit.js to focus textarea and press Control+Enter to submit message in Chainlit\"", "EndLine": "25", "Instruction": "\"Replace submission logic to focus textarea and press Control+Enter\"", "ReplacementContent": "\" await page.waitForSelector('textarea');\\n await page.screenshot({path: 'tests/manual/screenshots/chainlit1.png'});\\n console.log(\\\"Typing...\\\");\\n const textarea = await page.$('textarea');\\n await textarea.focus();\\n await page.type('textarea', 'use arxiv and search for papers on H-LSTMs');\\n await page.screenshot({path: 'tests/manual/screenshots/chainlit2.png'});\\n \\n console.log(\\\"Submitting...\\\");\\n await page.keyboard.down('Control');\\n await page.keyboard.press('Enter');\\n await page.keyboard.up('Control');\\n \\n console.log(\\\"Waiting for response...\\\");\\n await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 30000));\\n await page.screenshot({path: 'tests/manual/screenshots/chainlit3.png'});\\n await browser.close();\\n console.log(\\\"Done\\\");\"", "StartLine": "12", "TargetContent": "\" await page.waitForSelector('textarea');\\n await page.screenshot({path: 'tests/manual/screenshots/chainlit1.png'});\\n console.log(\\\"Typing...\\\");\\n await page.type('textarea', 'use arxiv and search for papers on H-LSTMs');\\n await page.screenshot({path: 'tests/manual/screenshots/chainlit2.png'});\\n \\n console.log(\\\"Submitting...\\\");\\n await page.click('#chat-submit');\\n \\n console.log(\\\"Waiting for response...\\\");\\n await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 30000));\\n await page.screenshot({path: 'tests/manual/screenshots/chainlit3.png'});\\n await browser.close();\\n console.log(\\\"Done\\\");\"", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/tests/manual/debug_chainlit.js\"", "toolAction": "\"Edit debug_chainlit.js\"", "toolSummary": "\"Edit debug_chainlit.js\"" }

1: # MCP_Explorer Walkthrough
2: 
3: ## Overview
4: I have successfully implemented the "MCP_Explorer" local-first multi-agent system. The system uses LangChain for orchestration, Chainlit for the UI, and connects to a local Ollama instance (`gemma4:e4b`).
5: 
6: > [!NOTE]
7: > All project code is located at: `[MCP_Explorer](file:///Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer)`
8: 
9: ## Key Components
10:

1: #!/bin/bash
2: set -e
3: 
4: echo "Setting up MCP_Explorer virtual environment..."
5: python3 -m venv venv
6: source venv/bin/activate
7: 
8: echo "Installing dependencies..."
9: pip install -e ".[test]"
10: 
11: echo "Installing playwright browsers..."
12: playwright install
13: 
14: echo "Setup complete! To run the application:"

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    I have successfully organized all the unorganized test files in the workspace. Here is a summary of the work done:

    1. File Organization

    I grouped the manual testing files into language-specific folders under tests/manual to keep the workspace clean: - Python Manual Tests (tests/manual/python/): - Moved e2e_test.py - Moved generate_trace.py - Moved generate_trace_decorator.py - Moved generate_trace_langchain.py - Moved generate_trace_simple.py - Moved verify_api.py - Moved verify_api2.py - Moved test_logging.py (originally located in the root scripts/ folder, which has now been deleted). - JavaScript Browser Tests (tests/manual/js/): - Moved debug_chainlit.js - Moved debug_chainlit_wiki.js - Moved mcp_test.js - Moved verify_puppeteer.js - Moved verify_puppeteer_fix.js - Moved verify_traces.js - Screenshots (tests/manual/screenshots/): - All temporary test screenshots (chainlit1.png, chainlit2.png, chainlit3.png) are located here.

    2. Path Adjustments & Fixes

    • Updated path loaders in tests/manual/python/generate_trace.py and tests/manual/python/test_logging.py to point to the correct project root directory (using parent.parent.parent.parent).
    • Corrected the Agent instantiation arguments in generate_trace.py to properly import CONFIG and load standard tools.
    • Fixed the Langfuse flushing logic in generate_trace.py and test_logging.py by calling _langfuse_client.flush() to resolve the AttributeError.
    • Updated test references and commands in walkthrough.md.

    3. Verification & Execution

    • Rebuilt the Docker compose stack using ./docker-run.sh to copy the new organized file structure into the app container.
    • Executed generate_trace.py and test_logging.py inside the container; they successfully ran and pushed traces to the local Langfuse dashboard without errors.
    • Ran the automated test suite using pytest --ignore=tests/manual. Fixed a test configuration failure in tests/test_config.py by monkeypatching the environment correctly. All 5 automated unit and integration tests are now passing.

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file

    json { "CodeContent": "\"# MCP_Explorer Walkthrough\\n\\n## Overview\\nI have successfully implemented the \\\"MCP_Explorer\\\" local-first multi-agent system. The system uses LangChain for orchestration, Chainlit for the UI, and connects to a local Ollama instance (`gemma4:e4b`).\\n\\n> [!NOTE]\\n> All project code is located at: `[MCP_Explorer](file:///Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer)`\\n\\n## Key Components\\n\\n### Architecture & Setup\\n- **Environment Management**: Implemented strict environment separation with `.env`, `.env.test`, and `.env.prod`. \\n- **Configuration Hub**: [config.py](file:///Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/config.py) serves as the single source of truth, loading variables dynamically based on the `MCP_ENV` system environment variable.\\n- **Agent Orchestration**: [core/agent.py](file:///Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/core/agent.py) defines a LangGraph `create_react_agent` that uses `ChatOllama` to talk to `http://localhost:11434`.\\n\\n### Dynamic Loading\\n- **MCP Servers**: [mcpConfig.json](file:///Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/mcpConfig.json) configures servers. [core/mcp_loader.py](file:///Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/core/mcp_loader.py) uses `langchain-mcp-adapters` to initialize the `MultiServerMCPClient` and keep the connection context active across requests.\\n- **Standard Tools**: [toolsConfig.json](file:///Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/toolsConfig.json) manages standard tools. [core/tool_loader.py](file:///Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/core/tool_loader.py) parses and initializes these standard LangChain tools using `load_tools()`.\\n\\n### User Interface\\n- **Chainlit App**: [ui/app.py](file:///Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/ui/app.py) handles the user chat session, managing tool initialization on start, creating the `Agent` object, and streaming token responses and tool usages directly to the browser.\\n\\n## Testing & Verification\\n\\n### End-to-End Test\\nI've provided a complete Playwright script ([e2e_test.py](file:///Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explo\n<truncated 3920 bytes>", "Description": "\"Write the updated walkthrough.md to the docs/ folder in the MCP_Explorer workspace.\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/walkthrough.md\"", "toolAction": "\"Write walkthrough.md to docs folder\"", "toolSummary": "\"Create walkthrough.md\"" }

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    I will create the .gitignore file in the project directory /Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/.gitignore to ignore Python cache files, node_modules/, the local .env file containing private credentials, and test screenshots.

    create readme.md in the project root

    I will create a comprehensive, premium-designed README.md in the project root directory /Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/README.md. It will document the architecture, features, directory structure, and instructions for running the application locally and via Docker.

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file

    json { "CodeContent": "\"# MCP Explorer \ud83d\ude80\\n\\nMCP Explorer is a local-first, multi-agent query and search system. It leverages LangChain and LangGraph for orchestration, Chainlit for a responsive chat UI, Ollama for local LLM inference (`gemma4:e4b`), and Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to load dynamic capabilities.\\n\\nAdditionally, the system features out-of-the-box observability with Langfuse and console trace logging.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83c\udfd7\ufe0f Architecture Overview\\n\\n\n \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u2502 Chainlit UI \u2502\n \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n \u2502\n \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25bc\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u2502 ReAct Agent \u2502\n \u2514\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2518\n \u2502 \u2502\n \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25bc\u2510 \u250c\u25bc\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u2502 Standard \u2502 \u2502 MCP Client \u2502\n \u2502 Tools \u2502 \u2502 (mcpConfig) \u2502\n \u2502 (toolsConfig\u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518 \u2502\n \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25bc\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u2502 SQLite / Web / \u2502\n \u2502 Custom MCP Server \u2502\n \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n\\n\\n- **Chat Interface**: Built with Chainlit (`ui/app.py`) for streaming token responses and tool-call rendering.\\n- **Agent Loop**: A ReAct agent (`core/agent.py`) powered by `ChatOllama` executing search, retrieval, and calculations.\\n- **Dynamic MCP Integrations**: Loads tools from any configured MCP servers via `mcpConfig.json` dynamically using `core/mcp_loader.py`.\\n- **Standard Integrations**: Parses standard tools (l\n<truncated 3662 bytes>", "Description": "\"Create README.md in the root directory detailing the overview, architecture, project structure, and run guides.\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile":     json { "CodeContent": "\"import json\\nimport re\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\ndef sanitize(text):\\n if not text:\\n return \\\"\\\"\\n # Case insensitive replacement of MCP with MCP\\n text = re.sub(r'(?i)MCP', 'MCP', text)\\n return text\\n\\ndef main():\\n log_path = Path(\\\"/Users/kathi.s/.gemini/antigravity/brain/a7dd681c-7ee8-4f02-8fa7-224242ec388f/.system_generated/logs/transcript.jsonl\\\")\\n output_path = Path(\\\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/session_history.md\\\")\\n \\n output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n \\n markdown = []\\n markdown.append(\\\"# MCP Explorer Development Session Log\\\\n\\\")\\n markdown.append(\\\"This document contains the chronological trace of the development session, showing how the MCP Explorer app was constructed, debugged, and optimized.\\\\n\\\")\\n \\n with open(log_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\\n for line in f:\\n if not line.strip():\\n continue\\n try:\\n step = json.loads(line)\\n source = step.get(\\\"source\\\")\\n step_type = step.get(\\\"type\\\")\\n content = step.get(\\\"content\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n created_at = step.get(\\\"created_at\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n \\n # Format timestamps a bit cleaner\\n if created_at:\\n created_at = created_at.replace(\\\"T\\\", \\\" \\\").split(\\\".\\\")[0]\\n \\n if step_type == \\\"USER_INPUT\\\":\\n # Parse out USER_REQUEST block from content if present\\n user_req_match = re.search(r'<USER_REQUEST>(.*?)</USER_REQUEST>', content, re.DOTALL)\\n if user_req_match:\\n req_content = user_req_match.group(1).strip()\\n else:\\n req_content = content.strip()\\n \\n if req_content:\\n markdown.append(f\\\"## \ud83d\udc64 User Request ({created_at})\\\\n\\\")\\n markdown.append(f\\\"> \n<truncated 1999 bytes>", "Description": "\"Create python script to parse development session log from transcript.jsonl into docs/session_history.md\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/tests/manual/python/parse_logs.py\"", "toolAction": "\"Create log parsing script\"", "toolSummary": "\"Create parse_logs.py\"" }

    json { "CodeContent": "\"site_name: MCP Explorer Documentation\\nsite_description: Comprehensive technical and developer documentation for the MCP Explorer agentic UI system\\nsite_author: Advanced Agentic Coding Team\\n\\ntheme:\\n name: material\\n palette:\\n - media: \\\"(prefers-color-scheme: dark)\\\"\\n scheme: slate\\n primary: deep purple\\n accent: deep purple\\n toggle:\\n icon: material/weather-night\\n name: Switch to light mode\\n - media: \\\"(prefers-color-scheme: light)\\\"\\n scheme: default\\n primary: deep purple\\n accent: deep purple\\n toggle:\\n icon: material/weather-sunny\\n name: Switch to dark mode\\n features:\\n - navigation.tabs\\n - navigation.sections\\n - navigation.top\\n - search.suggest\\n - search.highlight\\n - content.code.copy\\n\\nmarkdown_extensions:\\n - admonition\\n - pymdownx.details\\n - pymdownx.superfences\\n - pymdownx.highlight:\\n anchor_linenums: true\\n line_spans: __span\\n pygments_lang_class: true\\n - pymdownx.inlinehilite\\n\\nnav:\\n - Home: index.md\\n - Getting Started: setup.md\\n - Configuration: configuration.md\\n - Technical Architecture: architecture.md\\n - Component Breakdown: components.md\\n - Testing & Verification: testing.md\\n - Code Examples: examples.md\\n - Development History Log: session_history.md\\n\"", "Description": "\"Create mkdocs.yml file configuring the material theme, palettes, extensions, and navigation paths.\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/mkdocs.yml\"", "toolAction": "\"Create mkdocs.yml configuration\"", "toolSummary": "\"Create mkdocs.yml\"" }

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file

    json { "CodeContent": "\"# MCP Explorer\\n\\nWelcome to the **MCP Explorer** developer documentation!\\n\\nMCP Explorer is a state-of-the-art, local-first agentic query interface built with Python and LangChain. It uses a **ReAct (Reasoning and Acting)** loop powered by local large language models (such as `gemma4:e4b` via Ollama) to autonomously invoke tools, query external APIs, search databases, and format results.\\n\\nThe system is equipped with **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** clients to connect to remote or local MCP servers dynamically and query resources in real time.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83c\udf1f Key Features\\n\\n* **Local-First Agent Core**: Run LLMs locally using [Ollama](https://ollama.com) (defaulting to the `gemma4:e4b` model) with zero latency or remote API dependency.\\n* **Dynamic Model Context Protocol (MCP) Loader**: Automatically discover, load, and authenticate tools from multiple MCP servers dynamically using configurations in `mcpConfig.json`.\\n* **Standard Tools Extension**: Load and dynamically configure parameters (like custom user agents or endpoints) for classic tools (Wikipedia, Arxiv) defined dynamically in `toolsConfig.json`.\\n* **Interactive Chat UI**: A responsive, real-time streaming web dashboard built using **Chainlit** for developer conversations.\\n* **Observability Pipeline**: Automatic tracking of every message, prompt, LLM invocation, tool execution, token usage, and error boundary using **Langfuse**.\\n* **Dockerized Stack**: Run the entire system\u2014including database backends, workers, and visualization dashboards\u2014using a simple `./docker-run.sh` script.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\uddfa\ufe0f High-Level System Architecture\\n\\nThe workflow follows a standard ReAct loop:\\n\\n\n [User Inputs Query]\n \u2502\n \u25bc\n [Chainlit App Session]\n \u2502\n \u25bc\n [ReAct Agent] <\u2500\u2500\u2500(Reads Configs: env / toolsConfig.json)\n \u2502\n \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2534\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u25bc \u25bc\n[Run Standard] [Run MCP Server]\n [Tools] \n", "Description": "\"Create docs/index.md for home page of documentation.\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/index.md\"", "toolAction": "\"Create docs/index.md home page\"", "toolSummary": "\"Create docs/index.md\"" } ```

    • Tool: write_to_file

    json { "CodeContent": "\"# Getting Started & Setup Guide\\n\\nThis page explains how to install dependencies, configure environment settings, and run the MCP Explorer locally or inside a Docker Compose environment.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udcbb Method A: Running Locally (Bare Metal)\\n\\nRunning the application locally is ideal for debugging and editing components without container rebuilding.\\n\\n### Prerequisites\\n1. **Python 3.11+** installed on your system.\\n2. **Ollama** installed and running on `localhost:11434`. Pull the default model using:\\nbash\n ollama pull gemma4:e4b\n \\n\\n### 1. Execute Setup Script\\nWe provide an interactive `setup.sh` script to automate your local virtual environment instantiation:\\nbash\n./setup.sh\n\\nThis script will:\\n* Create a virtual environment (`venv`).\\n* Upgrade `pip` and install project packages declared in `pyproject.toml` (including automated test requirements).\\n* Install Playwright browser binaries for e2e automation testing.\\n\\n### 2. Configure Local Secrets\\nCreate a local `.env` file in the project root based on our template:\\nbash\ncp .env.example .env\n\\nOpen `.env` and fill in your values (e.g. your Langfuse credentials if using observability).\\n\\n### 3. Run the Chat Application\\nActivate the virtual environment and start Chainlit:\\nbash\nsource venv/bin/activate\nchainlit run ui/app.py\n\\nThe Chat UI will open automatically at: **[http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000)**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udc33 Method B: Running via Docker Compose Stack\\n\\nThe Docker Compose setup launches a complete production-ready pipeline, including clickhouse backends, redis caches, minio media uploads, postgres stores, langfuse-server logs visualization, and the chat app.\\n\\n### 1. Build and Run the Stack\\nWe provide a helper launch script `docker-run.sh` that auto-generates your secure encryption keys and starts the build:\\nbash\n./docker-run.sh\n\\nThis script:\\n* Checks for the existence of `.env`.\\n* Generates a safe `SALT`, `ENCRYPTION_KEY`, and `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` if not presen\n<truncated 1121 bytes>", "Description": "\"Create docs/setup.md detailing how to run locally and via Docker.\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/setup.md\"", "toolAction": "\"Create docs/setup.md setup guide\"", "toolSummary": "\"Create docs/setup.md\"" }

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    I will create docs/configuration.md detailing the env variables and configuration parameters used by the application, standard tools, and MCP servers.

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file

    json { "CodeContent": "\"# Configuration Reference Guide\\n\\nThis document describes all environment variables and configuration files (`toolsConfig.json` and `mcpConfig.json`) used to control the runtime behavior of the MCP Explorer.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udd11 Environment Variables\\n\\nThese variables are defined in your local `.env` file (for bare-metal execution) or `.env.docker` (in the Docker Stack).\\n\\n### Core Services Settings\\n\\n| Variable Name | Default Value | Description |\\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\\n| `OLLAMA_URL` | `http://localhost:11434` | The HTTP endpoint where your local Ollama server is running. Inside Docker containers, this is overridden to `http://host.docker.internal:11434`. |\\n| `MONGODB_URI` | `mongodb://localhost:27017/dev` | Connection URI for the MongoDB server used by the agent memory. |\\n| `LOG_LEVEL` | `DEBUG` | System verbosity logging level. Options are `DEBUG`, `INFO`, `WARNING`, `ERROR`, `CRITICAL`. |\\n\\n### Langfuse Observatory Connection\\n\\n| Variable Name | Target UI Location | Description |\\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\\n| `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY` | Settings -> API Credentials | Public API Key generated from the Langfuse project console to authenticate traces. |\\n| `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY` | Settings -> API Credentials | Secret API Key paired with the public key. Keep this private! |\\n| `LANGFUSE_HOST` | - | The HTTP host address of the Langfuse server. For local dev, this is `http://localhost:3000`. Inside Docker, the app container connects via `http://langfuse-server:3000`. |\\n\\n### Docker Stack Internal Secrets\\n\\nThese variables are auto-generated when running `./docker-run.sh` to secure your internal Postgres and session cookies:\\n\\n* `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`: Used by NextAuth to sign secure session tokens in the Langfuse dashboard.\\n* `SALT`: Secure string used by Langfuse to encrypt project variables.\\n* `ENCRYPTION_KEY`: A 64-character hexadecimal key used by the Langfuse backend to encrypt credential records stored in Postgres.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udee0\ufe0f Configuration Files\\n\\n### 1. `toolsConfig.json`\\nManages standard \n<truncated 1209 bytes>", "Description": "\"Create docs/configuration.md detailing env variables and json configs.\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/configuration.md\"", "toolAction": "\"Create docs/configuration.md reference guide\"", "toolSummary": "\"Create docs/configuration.md\"" }

    json { "CodeContent": "\"# Technical Architecture & Tracing\\n\\nThis page explains the internal mechanics of the MCP Explorer agentic system, covering ReAct loops, Model Context Protocol (MCP) transport, and observability integration.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udd01 The ReAct Agent Loop\\nThe agent core utilizes the **ReAct (Reasoning and Acting)** framework to solve tasks iteratively:\\n\\n1. **Reasoning**: The model (e.g. `gemma4:e4b`) analyzes the user's prompt and decides which tool (if any) to call, along with the required parameters.\\n2. **Acting**: The agent framework intercepts this decision and executes the tool locally or via MCP.\\n3. **Observation**: The tool output is appended to the message history, and the model evaluates if the answer is complete or if further actions are required.\\n\\nThis cycle continues dynamically until the model provides a final output.\\n\\n\n \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u2502 Human Input Message \u2502\n \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n \u2502\n \u25bc\n \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u25ba \u2502 LLM Reasoning Step \u2502\n \u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n \u2502 \u2502\n \u2502 [Decision]\n \u2502 / \\\n \u2502 [Tool Call] [Final Answer] \u2500\u2500\u25ba [Respond to User]\n \u2502 \u2502\n \u2502 \u25bc\n \u2502 \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u2502 \u2502 Tool Exec \u2502\n \u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n \u2502 \u2502\n \u2502 [Observation]\n \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udd0c Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration\\nThe Model Context Protocol allows the agent to communicate with separate external tools running as independent subprocesses.\\n\\n- **Process Isolation**: MCP s\n<truncated 1676 bytes>", "Description": "\"Create docs/architecture.md explaining ReAct loop, MCP, and Langfuse tracing.\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/architecture.md\"", "toolAction": "\"Create     🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file

    json { "CodeContent": "\"# Codebase Component Breakdown\\n\\nThis document provides a breakdown of each Python file in the MCP Explorer codebase, explaining its significance and architectural role.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udee0\ufe0f Configuration & Core Files\\n\\n### 1. `config.py`\\nThe central configuration hub of the application. It manages dynamic configuration reloading based on environments.\\n* **Significance**: Loads variables from `.env`, `.env.test`, or `.env.prod` depending on the value of the `MCP_ENV` environment variable (falling back to `.env` if undefined).\\n* **Key Objects**: `CONFIG` (dictionary housing `OLLAMA_URL`, `MONGODB_URI`, `LOG_LEVEL`, and pathways to tools/MCP configuration files).\\n\\n### 2. `core/agent.py`\\nThe brain of the system.\\n* **Significance**: Defines the custom `Agent` class wrapper. It instantiates `ChatOllama` utilizing the `gemma4:e4b` model and binds it to a list of dynamically resolved tools using LangGraph's `create_react_agent`.\\n* **Key Methods**:\\n * `ainvoke(prompt, thread_id, callbacks)`: Starts the agent as an asynchronous generator utilizing `astream_events` to yield granular execution events (model responses, token outputs, tool inputs/outputs) back to the caller.\\n\\n### 3. `core/logger.py`\\nThe diagnostic tracing console callback.\\n* **Significance**: Extends LangChain's `BaseCallbackHandler` to format and print colored, structured execution logs directly to the system CLI.\\n* **Key Methods**:\\n * `on_chain_start()` / `on_chain_end()`: Logs execution boundaries of sub-graphs.\\n * `on_tool_start()` / `on_tool_end()`: Traces the exact input and outputs of standard/MCP tool execution.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udd0c Loader Components\\n\\n### 4. `core/mcp_loader.py`\\nConnects the agent to external tool suites using the Model Context Protocol.\\n* **Significance**: Parses `mcpConfig.json`, spawns each configured MCP server (e.g. SQLite, local scripts) as an independent OS process, establishes stdin/stdout channels, and maps their exposed JSON-RPC tools into LangChain compatible tool interfaces.\n<truncated 1133 bytes>", "Description": "\"Create docs/components.md explaining each Python source file.\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/components.md\"", "toolAction": "\"Create docs/components.md code breakdown\"", "toolSummary": "\"Create docs/components.md\"" }

    • Tool: write_to_file

    json { "CodeContent": "\"# Testing & Verification Guide\\n\\nThis page explains how to run automated unit and integration tests, as well as execute manual verification scripts to check system integrity, agent loops, and observability tracing.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83e\uddea 1. Automated Testing (Pytest)\\n\\nWe use `pytest` (with `pytest-asyncio`) to run isolated configuration, loader, and integration tests.\\n\\n### Running the Tests\\nTo run the automated test suite, activate your virtual environment and execute `pytest`, ignoring the manual tests folder:\\nbash\nsource venv/bin/activate\npytest --ignore=tests/manual\n\\n\\n### Automated Test Suite Descriptions\\n* **`tests/test_config.py`**: Verifies that environment variable profiles (`default`, `test`, `prod`) reload variables (like `OLLAMA_URL` and `MONGODB_URI`) correctly from their respective `.env` files.\\n* **`tests/test_tool_loader.py`**: Validates the standard tools dynamic dynamic initializer, verifying the execution of Python code definitions inside `toolsConfig.json`.\\n* **`tests/test_integration.py`**: Asserts successful connectivity with a running Ollama container and verifies that the `Agent` is created with the expected endpoints.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udee0\ufe0f 2. Manual Testing Scripts (`tests/manual/`)\\n\\nManual test scripts are divided into language folders to separate browser automation tests from diagnostic script tests.\\n\\n### A. Python Verification Scripts (`tests/manual/python/`)\\n\\nThese scripts test specific API elements or simulate trace outputs directly inside the agent environment.\\n\\n* **`test_logging.py`**: Simulates two separate agent executions (one successful, one catching a tool division-by-zero boundary error) to verify that trace payloads and console logs are formatted correctly.\\n* **`generate_trace.py`**: Runs a simple query through the ReAct agent, flushes the Langfuse callback client, and confirms trace logs.\\n* **`generate_trace_simple.py`**: Directly invokes the Langfuse SDK to create a raw trace and span, verifying authorization keys.\\n* **`verify_api.py`\n<truncated 1124 bytes>", "Description": "\"Create docs/testing.md detailing unit testing and manual testing scripts.\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/testing.md\"", "toolAction": "\"Create docs/testing.md test reference\"", "toolSummary": "\"Create docs/testing.md\"" }

    json { "CodeContent": "\"# Code & Prompts Examples\\n\\nThis page provides practical examples of prompts, JSON configurations, and procedures for extending the capabilities of your MCP Explorer.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udcac 1. Example User Prompts\\n\\nYou can enter these queries into the Chainlit chat input to test the system's reasoning loop:\\n\\n### Standard Tools Queries\\n* *Prompt*: `use wikipedia and search for Rabindranath Tagore`\\n * **Agent Flow**: Decides to execute the `wikipedia` tool, scrapes the summary of Tagore, and uses the text to answer.\\n* *Prompt*: `use arxiv and search for papers on Transformer architectures`\\n * **Agent Flow**: Decides to run the `arxiv` tool, fetches recent publications matching the query, and formats the abstracts in Markdown.\\n\\n### Combined Tool Queries\\n* *Prompt*: `Search wikipedia for the formula of water, then search arxiv for recent papers detailing water purification techniques using that formula.`\\n * **Agent Flow**: Executes a sequential tool-calling chain (first Wikipedia, then uses the extracted data to formulate an Arxiv search query).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udd0c 2. Adding a Custom MCP Server\\n\\nYou can extend the agent's capabilities by adding server definitions to `mcpConfig.json`.\\n\\n### Example: SQLite MCP Server\\nTo allow the agent to read, write, and query a local database:\\n\\n1. Open `mcpConfig.json` in your workspace.\\n2. Add the SQLite server profile to the `mcpServers` object:\\njson\n {\n \\"mcpServers\\": {\n \\"sqlite\\": {\n \\"command\\": \\"uvx\\",\n \\"args\\": [\n \\"mcp-server-sqlite\\",\n \\"--db-path\\",\n \\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/dev.db\\"\n ]\n }\n }\n }\n \\n3. Restart your Chainlit server or rebuild the Docker container.\\n4. Ask the agent: `Show the tables in the SQLite database and list their schemas.` The agent will call the SQLite server tools dynamically.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udee0\ufe0f 3. Adding a Dynamic Tool Initialization\\n\\nIf a new tool requires specific conf\n<truncated 554 bytes>", "Description": "\"Create docs/examples.md detailing prompts and configs.\"", "IsArtifact": "false", "Overwrite": "true", "TargetFile": "\"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/examples.md\"", "toolAction": "\"Create docs/examples.md code reference\"", "toolSummary": "\"Create docs/examples.md\"" }

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    I have successfully set up the complete developer documentation site under the /docs folder using MkDocs (configured with the premium material theme), ensuring it is fully compatible with local serving and publication to GitHub Pages. All documentation references to the client project have been sanitized, replacing "MCP" with "MCP".

    Here is a summary of the files created and configured:

    1. MkDocs Configuration & Ignore Files

    • mkdocs.yml: Created in the root directory to define site metadata, color themes (light/dark adaptive mode), pymdown extensions for formatting, and page navigation structure.
    • .gitignore: Updated to ignore local compiled HTML site outputs (site/) to prevent repository bloating.

    2. Documentation Pages (under /docs)

    • index.md (Home / Overview): Features, benefits, and flow diagram of the MCP Explorer.
    • setup.md (Getting Started): Interactive step-by-step setup guides for both local execution and the Docker Compose stack.
    • configuration.md (Configuration Reference): Explains each environment variable and JSON config structure (mcpConfig.json, toolsConfig.json).
    • architecture.md (Architecture): Breakdown of the ReAct loops, JSON-RPC stdout/stdin communication of MCP servers, and Langfuse tracing.
    • components.md (Codebase Components): Descriptions of the functions and significance of all python source files (core/agent.py, core/mcp_loader.py, etc.).
    • testing.md (Testing Reference): Guide for automated testing (pytest) and running manual browser tests (Puppeteer/Playwright scripts).
    • examples.md (Code Examples): Prompting guides, dynamic tool inits, and steps for adding a custom SQLite MCP server.
    • walkthrough.md (Walkthrough): Sanitized implementation walkthrough explaining historical milestones.

    3. Log Parsing & Conversational Log

    • parse_logs.py: Created a Python script that reads the raw transcript.jsonl development log, extracts requests and responses, sanitizes the naming, and automatically formats them into a clean Markdown timeline.
    • session_history.md: Generated using the script, compiling the complete conversational history.

    4. Verification & Commit

    • Verified that the MkDocs site compiles cleanly without warnings or errors.
    • Staged all files and created a new Git commit. The repository's working tree is clean.

    👤 User Request (2026-06-05 14:01:30Z)

    how to run mkdocs?

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    To run the documentation site locally or build it for deployment, follow these steps:

    1. Install MkDocs

    Activate your virtual environment and install mkdocs along with the material design theme: 

    # Activate your local python virtual environment
source venv/bin/activate

# Install MkDocs and the Material theme
pip install mkdocs-material

    2. Run the Development Server (Local Preview)

    To preview the documentation site locally with live-reloading (automatically updates when you save markdown files): 

    mkdocs serve
    Once started, open your browser and navigate to: 👉 http://localhost:8000

    (If port 8000 is already in use by the Chainlit application, MkDocs will automatically use another port like 8001, or you can specify one manually: mkdocs serve -a localhost:8002)

    3. Build the Static HTML (For GitHub Pages/Hosting)

    To generate the static HTML and asset files for hosting (e.g. uploading to GitHub Pages, Netlify, or Vercel): 

    mkdocs build
    This will compile all Markdown files under /docs and output them to a new /site folder in the project root. (Note: /site is ignored in Git to keep the repository clean).
